title: Gratitude
description: Notice small things you appreciated. Save only when it feels useful - no streaks, no pressure.

home.title: Gratitude
home.subtitle: Notice what's good. Write it down. No pressure, no streaks.

welcome.title: The Science of Gratitude
welcome.subtitle: A short guide to what this practice is and why it works.
welcome.scienceBody: Acting selflessly and expressing gratitude activates specific brain regions — including the reward system and the cingulate cortex responsible for ethics and decision-making.
welcome.antidoteBody: Gratitude is one of the strongest predictors of happiness, the ability to make friends, and how well we handle hardship. A 2015 randomised trial found gratitude journaling produced significantly better mental health outcomes than psychotherapy alone.

levels.title: Three Levels of Practice
levels.subtitle: You can move between levels at any time. There is no wrong choice.
levels.level1Body: Simply report what happened today without judgment. A good meal, a calm moment, a conversation. Nothing is too small.
levels.level2Body: Think about what you would miss if it were gone. Notice things you usually take for granted — running water, sunlight, a friend nearby.
levels.level3Body: Write sentences starting with "I'm grateful for…" — for today and for your life. Aim for one to three times a week.

frequency.title: How Often?
frequency.subtitle: Research points to a sweet spot.
frequency.body: Researchers suggest writing one to three times a week rather than daily. Daily writing can lead to hedonic adaptation — the positive feeling fades as gratitude becomes routine.
frequency.note: There is no minimum. If you skip a few days, just pick it up again. You can't fail at this.

pick.title: Where Would You Like to Start?
pick.subtitle: You can change this any time from the home screen.
pick.level1Body: Report small daily events without judgment. Good for beginners or days when you have little energy.
pick.level2Body: Focus on what you would miss if it were gone. Builds on noticing.
pick.level3Body: Write "I'm grateful for…" sentences regularly. The most studied form of gratitude practice.
